feat: lock AssemblyVersion to major, FileVersion to full numeric version

Adds src/Directory.Build.targets (imported after SDK targets, so $(Version)
is fully resolved) with two overrides for all library projects:

  AssemblyVersion = major.0.0.0
    Locked to the major version so consumers compiled against v1.0 don't
    need to recompile for v1.1/v1.2 (binding-redirect / strong-name safety).

  FileVersion = major.minor.patch.0
    Pre-release label stripped (regex match on '^\d+\.\d+\.\d+') because
    Windows file-version fields only accept numeric components.

  InformationalVersion = x.y.z[-pre]+<commit-sha>
    Left to the SDK default — carries the full SemVer label plus source hash.

Verified against three cases:
  -p:Version=1.2.3      → AV=1.0.0.0  FV=1.2.3.0   IV=1.2.3+sha
  -p:Version=2.0.0-beta.1 → AV=2.0.0.0  FV=2.0.0.0   IV=2.0.0-beta.1+sha
  default (0.1.0)       → AV=0.0.0.0  FV=0.1.0.0   IV=0.1.0+sha

commit.message:
fix: break circular require between utils/init.lua and utils/cmp.lua

utils/init.lua (line 43) eagerly requires utils.cmp, which in turn
required utils at module load time — triggering a 'loop or previous
error loading module' error in LuaJIT before the utils table was
returned.

Fix: remove the module-level `local utils = require('utils')` from
utils/cmp.lua and inline `require('utils')` directly at the two call
sites (M.confirm closure, M.expand). Both sites are inside function
bodies, so by the time they execute package.loaded['utils'] is fully
populated and the require is a cheap cache hit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: improve code quality, performance and readability

Phase 1 — Remove `_G.Core` global
- Replaced all 147 `Core.*` references across 23 files with explicit
  local `require()` calls, eliminating the hidden global dependency
- Each file now declares its own minimal imports

Phase 2 — Split monolithic plugin files
- `plugins/lsp/config.lua`: 531 lines → 36-line aggregator
  - `servers/base.lua`     shared capabilities, diagnostics, on_init
  - `servers/lua_ls.lua`   Lua LSP config
  - `servers/typescript.lua` vtsls + typescript-tools + vtsls setup
  - `servers/python.lua`   ruff, pyright, pylsp, jedi + ruff setup
  - `servers/dotnet.lua`   omnisharp + roslyn
  - `servers/misc.lua`     prismals, powershell_es, copilot
- `plugins/ui.lua`: 643 lines → 13-line aggregator
  - `plugins/ui/nvchad.lua`   nvchad-ui specs
  - `plugins/ui/snacks.lua`   snacks.nvim (dashboard, picker, lazygit)
  - `plugins/ui/noice.lua`    noice.nvim + nui
  - `plugins/ui/trouble.lua`  trouble.nvim + todo-comments

Phase 3 — Error handling, dead code, quality
- Fixed 4 silent `pcall()` blocks (init.lua, coding.lua, navigation.lua,
  cmp.lua): now log warnings via `vim.notify` on failure
- Removed ~25 commented-out dead code lines from keymaps.lua, cmp.lua,
  navigation.lua; fixed "whick key" typo
- Documented `cond = false` plugins (oil.nvim, neo-tree) with reason comments
- Extracted window size magic numbers in `utils/ui.lua` into a `CONFIG`
  table (completion/doc max dims and viewport percentages)
- Deduplicated `DotnetPublish`/`DotnetBuild` commands via
  `make_dotnet_command()` helper in `plugins/lsp/cmd/dotnet.lua`

Phase 4 — LuaLS type annotations
- Added `@return` to `utils/fs.lua` (`get_root`, `find_root_marker`)
- Added `@return boolean` to `utils/shell.lua` (`is_linux`, `is_win`)
- Added `@return string` to `utils/str.lua` (`rstrip_slash`)

  `M.expand` in `utils/cmp.lua`
